articles.title: Risk analysis and information system security
articles.description: Entities must establish and maintain risk management frameworks, perform and document risk assessments, and implement risk treatment plans.

articles.title: Incident handling
articles.description: Establish and implement incident handling policies defining roles, responsibilities, and procedures for detecting, analysing, containing, responding to, and recovering from incidents.

articles.title: Business continuity and crisis management
articles.description: Backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management procedures to ensure continuity of operations.

articles.title: Supply chain security
articles.description: Security-related aspects of relationships with direct suppliers or service providers, including supply chain risk assessment.

articles.title: Security in acquisition, development and maintenance
articles.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development, and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

articles.title: Effectiveness assessment
articles.description: Policies and procedures to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures through audits, testing, and evaluation.

articles.title: Basic cyber hygiene and training
articles.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training for personnel.

articles.title: Cryptography and encryption
articles.description: Policies and procedures regarding use of cryptography and encryption to protect data confidentiality and integrity.

articles.title: HR security, access control and asset management
articles.description: Human resources security, access control policies, and asset management to restrict and monitor access to systems and data.

articles.title: MFA and secured communications
articles.description: Multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication, secured voice/video/text communications, and secured emergency communication systems.

articles.title: Reporting obligations
articles.description: Essential and important entities shall notify the CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident. Reporting follows a phased timeline: early warning, incident notification, intermediate report, and final report.

articles.title: Reporting — Early warning
articles.description: Within 24 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident. Must indicate if suspected unlawful/malicious cause and cross-border impact.

articles.title: Reporting — Incident notification
articles.description: Within 72 hours: update with initial assessment including severity, impact, and indicators of compromise.

articles.title: Reporting — Intermediate report
articles.description: Additional updates on status and handling as requested by CSIRT or competent authority.

articles.title: Reporting — Final report
articles.description: Within 1 month: detailed description, root cause, mitigation measures, and cross-border impact. Progress report if incident ongoing.
